text: Does a Gaussian wave packet v(x,0)=4 exp(-(x+5)^2/2.25), u(x,0)=0 in coupled Burgers-swept-KdV decompose into a soliton train (>=2 well-separated peaks, amp>=0.8, mass drift <8%) by T=6.0?

method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product (v^2, u*v, v*v_x, u*u_x) + classical explicit RK4 over full RHS (v_xxx kept explicit)
bank_use_rationale: E1 is the simplest meaningful baseline per progressive-complexity rules. Positive entry BKdV-S1 confirms Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealias + classical RK4 reached T=10 cleanly for amp up to 3.0 with dt=2e-4; I cut dt to 1e-4 because target amp=4 (33% higher than the validated range) and kb-gardner-G4/kb-gardner-nonlinearCFL-amplitude-boundary warn the nonlinear-CFL tightens as O(6A + 1.5A^2). I reject central-FD baselines (kb-burgers-fwdEuler-centralFD-Gibbs, kb-general-centralFD-hyperbolic-shockFormation, kb-shallowWater-centralFD-fwdEuler-hNegative) because no upwind dissipation on Burgers-like terms causes Gibbs/blow-up. I reject IFRK4 (kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup): exp(i k^3 t) overflows at high k. I reject no-dealiasing (kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts): would corrupt soliton count via aliased v^2. Bank does NOT permit me to escalate to MUSCL-Godunov-on-uu_x or IMEX-CN at E1: that would be a 2-3 component leap. Reserve IMEX-CN and MUSCL escalations for E2/E3 if E1 reveals stiffness or shock failure.

rationale: Phenomenon target met: final v has 10 well-separated peaks (distance>=10 grid points, prominence>=0.5) all with amplitude >=0.8 (min 0.82, max 1.80) -- vastly exceeds the >=2-peaks requirement. Mass drift is machine-precision-level (1e-14 relative), far below the 8% budget. Solver is well-resolved: only 6.8e-7 of energy near the dealias cutoff, confirming the 2/3-rule cleanly truncates aliasing. Behavior is consistent with KdV-type inverse-scattering: a large-amplitude (A=4) localized Gaussian is supercritical with respect to the soliton-formation threshold (kb-kdv-amplitude-threshold-soliton, kb-kdv-smallAmplitude-dispersiveRegime), so multiple solitons are shed. Spectral check (upper-band energy 6.8e-7) and mass-conservation check (1e-14) together rule out the failure modes flagged by kb-general-finiteness-not-accuracy and kb-general-massConservation-insufficient-diagnostic (these are not the only diagnostics; peak count, amplitudes, and spectral content all corroborate). Stopping early -- no need to escalate.

E1 baseline already meets the phenomenon target with strong margins; progressive-complexity discipline says do not escalate beyond what is needed. Bank entries kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-periodic-longTime-contamination (T=6 is below several domain-traversal times for L=30) and kb-general-massConservation-insufficient-diagnostic (peak count + amplitudes + spectral check were used in addition) both support stopping at E1.
